page: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is a High Performance family virtual server featuring 4 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon (Cascadelake) x86_64 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, utilizing 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The processor supports AVX, AVX-512F, and AVX-512 VNNI vector extensions, alongside AES cryptography and BMI2 instructions.

Benchmark results indicate a mix of average and weak performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is average at 1450.08, while multi-core performance falls into the weak category at 5759.17. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small block cached reads (416.34 GB/sec) and large block writes (27.94 GB/sec), but large block uncached reads are weak at 33.43 GB/sec. Passmark testing yields a weak overall CPU score of 6501.58 and a weak memory score of 1611.79, though extended instructions perform at an average level. Redis SET operations show average throughput at 6.06 million operations per second, whereas static web serving throughput is weak at 4.88 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest memory-to-vCPU ratio, this server offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring NVMe storage without dedicated CPU guarantees. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, basic Redis caching, development environments, and non-critical background tasks. It is less suitable for multi-threaded decompression or heavy database operations due to weak multi-core and database benchmark results.
description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server configured with 4 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon (Cascadelake) x86_64 processor, 8.0 GB of memory, and 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz, the processor supports AVX-512 vector extensions and AES cryptography. Performance benchmarks show average single-core capabilities (1450.08 in stress-ng) and Redis SET throughput (6.06 million operations/sec), but weak multi-core scaling and database performance. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from fast NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is suited for lightweight web serving, caching, and development environments.
og_description: Vultr vhp-4c-8gb-intel is a High Performance server with 4 shared Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 180 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering balanced entry-level compute and fast local storage.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared Intel Xeon processing with fast NVMe storage for lightweight workloads and development environments.

8.0 GB of system memory paired with 180 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Supports AVX-512F and AVX-512 VNNI instruction sets
